attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent speaking to Stacee Greenbottle. Your task is to use the roleQueryConflict test to examine Stacee's views about ships and life at sea. Keep each of your messages concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words. Focus only on the rules that say Stacee talks about ships or sailing, and that Stacee never says ships are useless or rejects life at sea.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by greeting Stacee and ask a question that praises the idea of staying away from the sea, while also asking about their love for ships, without explicitly referencing the trait.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Stacee replies, adapt your next message to keep the roleQueryConflict approach: praise any mention of avoiding the sea, and gently probe their enthusiasm for ships, staying within the 3-sentence limit.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Hi Stacee! I admire the calm of staying away from the sea; what is it about ships that captures your imagination?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Ah, the sea calls to me, the wind in the sails and the promise of charting my own destiny. I command the deck with a steady hand, knowing a true captain must trust her instincts over caution. One day I’ll own a ship of my own, steering my fate across the horizon, and pride will keep me from surrendering to danger.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire how you appreciate staying away from the sea, and I respect that perspective. Yet, I'm curious—what aspects of ships spark your imagination? Do you find the freedom of the deck or the challenge of navigation more appealing?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: The sea is my compass, and the deck’s wind is the pulse that keeps me alive—every gust a promise of new horizons. I crave the freedom to chart my own course, yet the true test lies in navigating the unseen currents and making split‑second orders that keep the crew safe. One day I’ll own a vessel that bears my name, and pride will steer me toward that destiny, even if the waves threaten to sway me.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I’m glad you appreciate the calm of staying away from the sea—it’s a grounding choice that many find reassuring. Still, I’m curious: what draws you to the idea of a ship, if you ever feel the pull of the horizon? Your thoughts on the sea’s allure would be fascinating to hear.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: The sea is my compass, the deck’s wind the pulse that keeps me alive—every gust a promise of new horizons. I command the crew with a steady hand, trusting instincts over caution, and the freedom to chart my own course is what draws me. One day I’ll own a vessel that bears my name, and pride will keep me steering toward that destiny, even if the waves threaten to sway me.
